key_points: The Median is the middle observation after arranging data in order
The Mode is the value that occurs most frequently
Double Bar Graphs are used for the simultaneous comparison of two data sets
Probability measures the chance of an event occurring (ranges from 0 to 1)
examples: Median: For [3, 5, 7, 10, 12], the middle value is 7
Mode: In [2, 3, 3, 4, 5, 3], the mode is 3 (appears most)
Double Bar Graph: Comparing Boy/Girl population across different classes
Probability: Chance of getting a 'Tail' in a coin flip is $1 \div 2 = 0.5$
formulas: Probability $\rightarrow \frac{\text{Number of favourable outcomes}}{\text{Total number of possible outcomes}}$
exam_tips: NEVER calculate the median without first arranging the data in ascending or descending order
A dataset can have more than one mode (Bimodal) or no mode at all
In probability, an 'Impossible' event is 0 and a 'Certain' event is 1
Double bar graphs must have a legend to distinguish between the two categories
